title: strychnos elaeocarpa
intro: Strychnos elaeocarpa is a species of flowering plant in the family Loganiaceae, native to Australia. The plant has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antifungal activities. The health benefits of Strychnos elaeocarpa include its potential to treat various conditions such as fever, rheumatism, and skin infections, as well as its use as an antiseptic and anti-inflammatory agent. The herbal preparations of Strychnos elaeocarpa include infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, which are typically made from the leaves, bark, or roots of the plant.
